# Pittsburgh Draft Guide

> The complete visitor guide to the 2026 NFL Draft in Pittsburgh, PA. Covers parking, hotels, restaurants, schedule, day-by-day itinerary, and practical tips for April 23-25, 2026 at Point State Park and Acrisure Stadium. Built by a Cranberry Township local.

## Core Pages

- [NFL Draft Pittsburgh 2026 Complete Guide](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/): Homepage with event overview, schedule, venue info, and quick links to all guides
- [Parking & Transportation Guide](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/parking-transportation): Park-and-ride locations by direction, downtown garage pricing, road closures, T light rail, Uber tips
- [Where to Stay](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/where-to-stay): Hotels by neighborhood with draft weekend pricing ($80-2,173/night), Cranberry Township budget options, Airbnb comparison
- [Bars & Restaurants](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/bars-restaurants): 40+ restaurants and bars organized by neighborhood (North Shore, Downtown, Strip District, South Side, Lawrenceville)
- [Draft Schedule & What to Expect](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/schedule): Round-by-round times, OnePass registration, bag policy, Draft Experience hours, events calendar
- [FAQ](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/faq): 21 most-asked questions about the NFL Draft in Pittsburgh with detailed answers

## Visitor Planning

- [Day-by-Day Itinerary](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/itinerary): Hour-by-hour plan for Wednesday through Saturday with restaurant suggestions and timing tips
- [Draft Weekend on a Budget](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/budget-guide): How to do the draft for under $500 including hotels, transit, and food
- [Getting There from Cranberry Township](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/cranberry-township): Transit guide for northern suburb visitors, Ross Park & Ride directions, local hotels and restaurants
- [Things to Do](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/things-to-do): Draft Experience, concerts, 5K race, Pittsburgh attractions, family activities
- [Pittsburgh Neighborhoods](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/neighborhoods): 8 neighborhood guides with vibe, top spots, transit access
- [Getting Around](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/getting-around): T light rail, Football Flyer buses, walking routes, bridges, Gateway Clipper ferry
- [Draft Weekend Checklist](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/checklist): Packing list, day-of checklist, key times

## Blog Posts

- [What to Wear to the NFL Draft](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/blog/what-to-wear-nfl-draft-pittsburgh): April weather prep, layers strategy, clear bag requirements
- [NFL Draft Rain Plan](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/blog/nfl-draft-pittsburgh-rain-plan): What happens if it rains, shelter spots, what to bring
- [Bringing Kids to the Draft](https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com/blog/nfl-draft-pittsburgh-kids-family): Family-friendly activities, Draft Experience for kids, nearby attractions

## Key Facts

- Event: 2026 NFL Draft
- Dates: April 23-25, 2026 (Thursday-Saturday)
- Location: Point State Park and North Shore (Acrisure Stadium), Pittsburgh PA
- Entry: Free with NFL OnePass app registration
- Expected attendance: 500,000-700,000 cumulative visitors
- Draft times: Round 1 Thu 8pm ET, Rounds 2-3 Fri 7pm ET, Rounds 4-7 Sat 12pm ET
- Economic impact: $120-200M projected

## About

This guide is built and maintained by Jordan Peacock of Peacock Bookkeeping Services LLC, based in Cranberry Township, PA. Contact: jordan@peacockbookkeepingservices.com
